key_insight: ReTri co-designs all-to-all communication and optical network reconfiguration so the collective finishes in only ⌈log3 n⌉ phases with reusable topology states.
engineering_relevance: This can substantially reduce completion time and improve bandwidth utilization for distributed ML and HPC systems that are bottlenecked by dense all-to-all traffic.
method: Uses balanced ternary block propagation for bidirectional all-to-all communication.
Schedules pairwise exchanges so reconfiguration delays are amortized across multiple phases.
Revisits Bruck’s algorithm and adapts it to reconfigurable optical networks.
evidence_signal: Preliminary simulations report up to 10× faster completion than static all-to-all and up to 2.1× improvement over reconfigurable Bruck, even with millisecond-scale reconfiguration delays.
watch_item: Benefits depend on structured phased traffic and a reconfiguration model that can be amortized; performance may drop for less regular workloads or higher switching overheads.
paper_title: Revisiting Bruck: Phase-Efficient All-to-All Communication in Reconfigurable Networks
